desc: Varna is Bulgaria's maritime capital — a laid-back Black Sea city where Roman ruins, Belle Époque facades and long urban beaches meet one of the country's best food scenes. The Sea Garden stretches for kilometres along the coast, while the Archaeological Museum holds the world's oldest processed gold from the Varna Necropolis. In summer, Varna is a lively beach base with easy access to quiet coves, coastal monasteries and the dramatic stone forest formations inland.

todo.name: Sea Garden (Primorski Park)
todo.desc: Varna's green coastal spine with sea views, cafes, gardens and open-air cultural venues. Ideal for sunset walks.

todo.name: Varna Archaeological Museum
todo.desc: Home of the Varna Gold (5th millennium BC), the oldest known worked gold in the world.

todo.name: Roman Thermae
todo.desc: One of the largest preserved Roman bath complexes in the Balkans, right in the city centre.

todo.name: Varna Cathedral
todo.desc: The Dormition of the Mother of God Cathedral is Varna's iconic skyline landmark with ornate Orthodox interiors.

todo.name: Aladzha Monastery
todo.desc: A medieval cave monastery carved into limestone cliffs in the forest north of the city.

todo.name: Golden Sands day trip
todo.desc: Popular resort strip 25–30 minutes from Varna with broad sandy beaches and lively summer nightlife.

todo.name: Stone Forest (Pobiti Kamani)
todo.desc: Strange desert-like field of natural stone columns, one of Bulgaria's most unusual landscapes.

todo.name: Balchik & Cape Kaliakra
todo.desc: A rewarding full-day coastal drive for clifftop views, botanical gardens and dramatic Black Sea headlands.

wifi.note: Reliable broadband across central Varna and beach districts, with strong 4G/5G coverage and many laptop-friendly cafes.

prac.fltNote: No routine direct LGW → Varna service year-round. Typical options are one-stop flights via Sofia or major European hubs; seasonal UK charters can appear in peak summer.
prac.visa: Visa-free for UK citizens — up to 90 days in any 180-day period
alerts: July and August are the busiest months on the Black Sea coast — book beachfront stays early for best value.
Some beach clubs and seasonal restaurants close quickly after mid-September outside school-holiday periods.

sweet: June and September are the sweet spot — warm sea temperatures and beach weather with fewer crowds than July–August.
